title: Grizzly Bear Attack
intro: A massive grizzly bursts from willow thickets at a dead charge. Lewis later writes: 'I had rather fight two Indians than one bear.'

choices.label: Stand and volley — drive it off
choices.text: Six balls later, the bear finally drops. Private Shannon is clawed badly but will recover. The corps feasts on bear meat.

choices.label: Scatter into the river
choices.text: The men dive in all directions. The bear mauls one man before losing interest. York drags him to safety.

choices.label: Drouillard steps forward
choices.text: Drouillard, cool as January ice, draws the bear away and dispatches it with one shot. The corps watches in stunned silence.

title: Vast Buffalo Herd
intro: The horizon blackens. Clark estimates ten million buffalo — three days to pass. This is Jefferson's Louisiana in all its wild abundance.

choices.label: Lay over and hunt — fill every barrel
choices.text: Three days of magnificent hunting. Every container is packed. Drouillard alone brings in eleven.

choices.label: Take only what you can carry and press on
choices.text: Five animals — enough for a week — and you continue. Time is precious.

choices.label: Drive a small herd toward camp
choices.text: York organizes a clever drive. The corps takes twelve animals in two hours without ranging far.

title: Teton Sioux Confrontation
intro: Three Teton Sioux warriors seize the bow rope of a pirogue. Two hundred warriors line the shore. Black Buffalo and The Partisan watch. The captains' hands move to their swords.

choices.label: Stand firm — draw weapons
choices.text: Both captains draw swords. The swivel gun swings toward shore. A tense hour passes. Black Buffalo intervenes and the warriors withdraw.

choices.label: Offer gifts and Jefferson Medals
choices.text: You present tobacco and medals. The Partisan is unsatisfied but Black Buffalo accepts. Tense — but bloodless.

choices.label: York walks to the bow
choices.text: York addresses the warriors directly. His appearance transfixes them. The confrontation dissolves into astonished, peaceful questions.

choices.label: Sacagawea speaks to a warrior she recognizes
choices.text: Sacagawea quietly points out a warrior who is her distant kinsman. The recognition changes the entire atmosphere.

title: Dangerous River Crossing
intro: The Missouri runs wide here — three hundred yards of fast, cold current. The keelboat is heavy with stores. One bad decision and the expedition ends in the mud.

choices.label: Ferry everything across systematically
choices.text: It takes two full days, but every man, rifle, and barrel makes it safely. Methodical and sure.

choices.label: Ride the current — attempt a direct crossing
choices.text: The keelboat nearly founders twice. A crate of gunpowder goes over. But you cross in four hours.

choices.label: Find a ford upstream with Drouillard
choices.text: Drouillard ranges three miles and finds a natural ford at a gravel bar. The crossing costs nothing.

choices.label: Hire native guides to show safe passage
choices.text: A Mandan family shows you a channel route. You cross safely and part as friends.

title: Blizzard on the Plains
intro: Temperature drops forty below. Men's beards freeze solid. The Mandan warned of winters like this. The thermometer cannot measure it.

choices.label: Dig in and build shelters
choices.text: Four days lost but the corps is saved. Frostbite claims two toes from Whitehouse.

choices.label: Push on — generate body heat
choices.text: Brutal. Two men collapse and must be carried. But you find timber by nightfall.

choices.label: Sacagawea negotiates shelter
choices.text: She goes alone into the storm and returns with a Hidatsa family who shelter the corps for four days.

title: Rattlesnake Strikes Lewis
intro: Captain Lewis is bitten on the ankle collecting specimens. The leg swells alarmingly. Seaman, his dog, barks frantically.

choices.label: Apply Rush's bark and rest two days
choices.text: Lewis doses himself and rests. Pale and feverish, he recovers — losing two days of travel.

choices.label: Sacagawea treats with a poultice
choices.text: Sacagawea prepares a remedy from plants gathered within the hour. Lewis is on his feet by morning.

choices.label: Keep marching — Lewis refuses to stop
choices.text: He marches on between bouts of nausea, dictating journal entries. He survives — barely.

title: Grand Council with the Mandan
intro: The Mandan chiefs invite the captains to a great council. Pipe smoke fills the circular earthlodge. Much can be gained here — or lost by a wrong word.

choices.label: Deliver Jefferson's speech of sovereignty
choices.text: You read Jefferson's words in translation. The chiefs listen gravely and respond with equal dignity.

choices.label: Ask about the route west
choices.text: You focus on the mountains ahead. The chiefs produce excellent maps drawn in the dirt.

choices.label: York performs and sings
choices.text: York dances and sings. The Mandan are delighted beyond measure. Chief Big White promises guides for spring.

title: Shoshone Horse Council
intro: You have found the Shoshone — and their chief is Sacagawea's brother Cameahwait, whom she has not seen in years. The reunion is profoundly moving.

choices.label: Sacagawea negotiates directly
choices.text: Sacagawea and Cameahwait speak for hours. Twenty-nine horses and a mountain guide. Lewis weeps openly.

choices.label: Offer Jefferson Medals and iron goods
choices.text: A fair trade. You receive horses and Cameahwait agrees to guide you to the pass.

title: The Great Falls Portage
intro: Five waterfalls block the Missouri. An eighteen-mile portage under scorching sun. Lewis calls it 'the most laborious business I have ever witnessed.'

choices.label: Build cottonwood wagon wheels
choices.text: York leads construction. The eighteen-mile portage takes twenty-four days. Men's feet bleed through their moccasins.

choices.label: Cache supplies — carry only essentials
choices.text: You bury non-essentials and carry only what you need. Fourteen days. A cache awaits on the return.

title: Dysentery Strikes the Corps
intro: Half the corps is stricken. The captains administer Dr. Rush's famous Thunderclappers. The camp becomes a grim, odorous place.

choices.label: Rest for three days — full recovery
choices.text: The expedition halts. By the third day most men are recovered, though weakened.

choices.label: Sacagawea brews medicinal tea
choices.text: Sacagawea prepares a tea from dried herbs. The men recover in half the time, astonishing the captains.

choices.label: Press on — the strong will endure
choices.text: You push the march. Three more men fall ill. The sick are carried on litters. Brutal but you maintain pace.

title: Successful Hunt
intro: Drouillard and Colter return from a twelve-mile range in high spirits. The smell of roasting elk fills the camp as stars appear.

choices.label: Feast and rest — full morale boost
choices.text: You order a full feast. The men sing, tell stories, sleep well for the first time in weeks.

choices.label: Preserve and cache the surplus
choices.text: You spend a day jerking meat and rendering fat. The cache will sustain the return journey.

choices.label: Drouillard leads a second hunt at dawn
choices.text: He ranges again and brings in six more animals. A legendary two-day hunt.

title: Lost in the Bitterroots
intro: Snow falls in August. The trail vanishes. Men eat tallow candles and boiled moccasins. This is the moment the expedition nearly dies.

choices.label: Push forward on half rations
choices.text: Gaunt and hollow-eyed, the corps pushes through eight desperate days. Nez Perce find you on the Weippe Prairie.

choices.label: Sacagawea recognizes a childhood landmark
choices.text: She leads the party through a Shoshone pass unknown to any map. You emerge two days sooner.

choices.label: Send Drouillard ahead to find the Nez Perce
choices.text: He ranges far and returns with a Nez Perce elder willing to guide the party.

title: New Species Discovered
intro: Lewis spends the day in barely contained scientific ecstasy — prairie dogs, magpies, strange cacti, plants unknown to any botanist.

choices.label: Document everything — spend two days
choices.text: Lewis fills three notebooks. The specimens are carefully preserved. This will astonish Philadelphia.

choices.label: Sketch quickly and keep marching
choices.text: Lewis sketches rapidly as the corps moves. Rougher notes, but the expedition maintains pace.

title: Vista Beyond Reckoning
intro: The corps crests a ridge and stops dead. Mountains, valleys, rivers — all stretching to every horizon. No American has ever described this country.

choices.label: Camp here — absorb the sublime sight
choices.text: Men who haven't spoken in days grow animated. Clark begins a new map. Morale is transformed.

choices.label: Descend at once — daylight is precious
choices.text: The sight sustains every man through the descent. Lewis writes: 'I felt rewarded for every difficulty.'

title: Missouri in Spring Flood
intro: The winter snowmelt has turned the Missouri into a brown monster. Entire trees wheel past in the current. The keelboat is nearly swamped three times in an hour.

choices.label: Make for shore and wait out the flood
choices.text: You lose five days camped on high ground, watching the river rage. When it recedes the going is easier.

choices.label: Pole hard — push through the high water
choices.text: The men labor at the poles for sixteen hours straight. Three are swept overboard but recovered.

choices.label: Sacagawea points to a calmer side channel
choices.text: She grew up reading rivers like this. Her shortcut avoids the worst of the current entirely.

title: The Prairie Dog Town
intro: Lewis and Clark discover an enormous colony of small barking squirrels unlike anything in American natural history.

choices.label: Spend a day trapping a live specimen
choices.text: The men pour barrel after barrel of water down the burrows. A dazed animal emerges — it will eventually reach Jefferson alive.

choices.label: Document and sketch — keep moving
choices.text: Lewis sketches furiously. His notes are meticulous. Science will be delighted.

choices.label: Let Seaman investigate
choices.text: Lewis's Newfoundland dog plunges headfirst into a burrow. He emerges muddy, baffled, and tremendously pleased. The corps howls with laughter.

title: Hostile Arikara Villages
intro: Three Arikara villages line the bank. Warriors watch from the shore with rifles. The Arikara have been hostile to all river traffic this season.

choices.label: Anchor mid-river and hail them peacefully
choices.text: Lewis reads Jefferson's speech of friendship. The Arikara chief rows out, suspicious but listening. Uneasy peace holds.

choices.label: Pass by without stopping — show no fear
choices.text: Both captains stand at the bow in full uniform. The warriors watch but do not fire.

choices.label: Drouillard speaks in sign language from the bow
choices.text: His calm, respectful signs lower the tension. Warriors begin laughing. Crisis averted.

choices.label: Offer tobacco and trade goods
choices.text: You send gifts across in a small pirogue. The chief accepts and allows passage.

title: Cruzatte Plays the Fiddle
intro: Pierre Cruzatte — one-eyed, half-Omaha, the finest boatman on the river — produces his fiddle at dusk. He plays for two hours straight.

choices.label: Dance and celebrate — full evening of rest
choices.text: The men dance themselves to exhaustion. Private Potts does a jig that brings the house down. Everyone sleeps magnificently.

choices.label: Keep it short — early march tomorrow
choices.text: An hour of music and then to bed. The men are grateful and rested.

title: Horses Stolen in the Night
intro: Dawn reveals disaster: the picket pins pulled free in the night. Three horses are gone — likely taken by Crow raiders.

choices.label: Pursue the raiders — Drouillard tracks them
choices.text: Drouillard follows the trail for eight miles and negotiates the return of two horses.

choices.label: Accept the loss and redistribute loads
choices.text: Bitter. The remaining horses are burdened heavier. Progress slows.

choices.label: Trade for replacement horses
choices.text: A nearby Shoshone family agrees to trade. You pay well but cannot afford to march horseless.
